documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/CrackMapExec && python3 CrackMapExec.py --help`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/CrackMapExec && cat README.md`; `cd toolboxes/CrackMapExec && ls -la`. This project is no longer mantained due to the existence of a hostile fork.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: This project is no longer mantained due to the existence of a hostile fork.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: This project is no longer mantained due to the existence of a hostile fork.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the Penetration Testing category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the Penetration Testing system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
